title: G.SKILL USB KM360 Professional Tenkeyless Mechanical Keyboard, Cherry MX Red, ABS Dual Injection Keycap, (Black)
description: The G.SKILL KM360 tenkeyless mechanical keyboard is a compact 87-key layout keyboard designed for durability. Featuring long-lasting Cherry MX mechanical keyswitches, wear-proof ABS double injection keycaps, and a solid aluminum top-plate, the KM360 mechanical keyboard is the perfect daily mechanical keyboard. With a detachable USB Type-C cable, it's easy to replace a worn cable by simply swapping it out with another USB Type-C cable of your choice.
features: CHERRY MX - Most popular mechanical keyswitch for their high standards of quality, reliability, and durability; rated for over 50 million keypresses.
COMPACT & PORTABLE - Designed with a detachable USB Type-C cable and a tenkeyless layout for more desk space and great portability.
WEAR-PROOF KEYS - Using ABS dual-injection keycaps mean the keys won't wear out like printed or laser etched keycaps.
HIGH DURABILITY - Constructed with a solid aluminum top plate and floating keyswitches for easy cleaning.
MODERN DESIGN - Featuring ergonomic keys, white LED backlight, and n-key rollover for comfort and ease of use.

reviews.title: Clicky clack who’s at the rack with the jackets? Me!
reviews.text:
I had been eyeing a mechanical keyboard for years, hesitant to buy one because A) I already had a perfectly fine working keyboard and B) Reviews I had read made it sound like they were so noisy you needed to wear ear protection while using one. During a recent move I left my previously mentioned perfectly fine working keyboard outside for an extended period of time, uncovered in the rain and sun and any other elements that rolled though Southern California for those few months. When I finally returned to the keyboard, I found that it had become yet another casualty of the pandemic. Finally I had good cause for a new keyboard, and my research led me to try this one. And like the famous tattoo says, I have no ragrets!

I plugged it in and immediately it lit up and off my little fingies went, zipping around the keyboard with the grace and elegance of a gold medal winning gymnast at the 2021 Tokyo Olympics. Every click and clack was like music to my ears, each sentence I typed sounding like a symphony composed by Mozart himself! I now look forward to typing my weekly emails to mother, catching her up with her favorite son’s (sorry Richard, but you know it’s true!) comings and goings!
I’ve only had this keyboard for about a week, so time will tell if it will hold up to the rigorous poundings that accompany each email I compose, but as of now I would not hesitate to recommend this keyboard to all my many (ok, zero) friends! Now I can’t wait to get off from my day job as a clerk typist and go home and get some typing in!

reviews.title: Great Initial Impression
reviews.text:
My first foray into mechanical KB and cherry switches. I was familiar with GSkill as they are a trusted brand in performance PC DDR4 RAM. I liked the price point and the size was perfect. Smaller than a full size but still having arrow keys and delete, etc off to the side.
I’m no KB expert, but it appears to be of good quality and finish. Nice adjustable LED backlighting. It has a sleeved nylon cable that’s white and matches the board nicely. Typing on it will take some getting used to, but I’m liking it so far and will update later if there’s any issues.

reviews.title: Amazing keyboard EDIT:
reviews.text: Amazing keyboard.  I'm typing on my new G.SKILL KM360 Professional Tenkeyless Mechanical Keyboard and YES it amazing!  Seriously if you have the money by this product.  Totally worth every penny! It's been just over a year since I got this mechanical keyboard & the USB type C is coming loose, I have to move the cord back N forth so it will connect again. Not sure if it can be fixed or not. (EDIT) I got this in October of 2019 & the small side of the USB Type-C female side is breaking off, causing the keyboard to flicker on and off. Other than that it's awesome keyboard

reviews.title: Great keyboard from a regular joe
reviews.text:
This is my first keyboard and saw great review. I’m not a big gamer or anything but recently started playing a little on a gaming laptop and thought why not. I thought the keyboard on the laptop were fine but damn these are better. I started using them for work and everything and love this key board now. So if your on the fence just do it.

10/10

reviews.title: Almost perfect
reviews.text: Exactly what I was looking for in an TKL board. The only thing that’s below standard are the kick stands. They don’t provide much resistance, so when you just nudge the keyboard forward the kickstands collapse - not sure if they need to be more sturdy or have a more grippy rubber foot but a rather disappointing feature to an otherwise perfect set up.

title: Gliging 9009 Keycaps Cherry Profile Dye Sublimation 9009 Keycap ANSI Thick PBT for Mechanical Keyboard 61 64 68 87 104 108
description: Warm Tips：It’s only keycaps, no keyboard included. The keyboard is only for taking photo,not included in the sold package.
features: Only Keycaps：It’s only keycaps, no keyboard included. The keyboard is only for taking photo,not included in the sold package.
Multiple Colors：There are two additional colors for the key, you can DIY the color of the keycap according to the keyboard style.
Dye Sublimation：The keycaps are made of durable and anti-grease PBT material. Compared with ABS, PBT has better high hardness, wear resistance and high temperature resistance.

Wide Selection of Keycaps：Consists of 139 keycaps and other stabilizers, suitable for 90% of 61/87/104/108 mechanical and optical keyboards, as well as any key switches with cross-shaped shafts, ideal for custom mechanical keyboards select.
Warm Tips：If the spacebar is 6u 6.5u instead of 6.25u,it can't be compatible, please read carefully before order.

reviews.title: Good quality, thick keycaps
reviews.text: Fits my case perfectly. Feels nice and looks nice.

reviews.title: Good quality and adorable!
reviews.text:
Key caps are textured, but do not feel rough. The textured PBT will keep them from getting shiny as fast (compared to smooth ABS). No print errors. Fit the Cherry switches perfectly. They feel weighty and durable. The plastic is cut cleanly, no rough edges. Nice big font, centered on each key. I am very happy with these.

The only thing I am a bit disappointed about is that the special (non-standard) Capslock key does not fit my keyboard; the slot where you are supposed to insert the switch is not centered correctly. But, it isn't enough of a disappointment for me to knock down my rating.

reviews.title: Perfect!
reviews.text: These keycaps are made of PBT, which is about the highest grade of plastic that you can get for your keycaps. I love the flatter profile of the XDA keycaps. The print on the keys is very classic, large, and easy to read. This keycap set contains both peach and sea green keycaps for the function keys, space bar, escape, and enter keys. You can mix and match them until your hearts delight. You even get some extra keys for media keys if your keyboard has them, plus menu and function keys. You get a wide variety of keys that will fit most Cherry MX style keyboards easily. The plastic is nice and textured so your fingers won't slip off the keycaps when you are typing on the keyboard. I would highly recommend these keycaps as an upgrade to ABS keycaps, and for customizing your keyboard in a cute vintage style.

title: KEMOVE Maple Theme Keycaps, Thermal Sublimation PBT Keycap 70 Key Set, XDA Profile ANSI Layout for 60% Mechanical Keyboard, English (US) Layout-Orange White
features: ✅【60% keycaps】This Maple custom keycap set consists of 70 keycaps, compatible with 60% MX mechanical keyboards, applicable keyboard models: GANSS ALT61 series, RK61 series, IKBC poker series, Anne PRO, GH60, iqunix f60, GK64 GK61, etc., perfect for custom mechanical keyboard selection.
✅【XDA Profile Keycaps】XDA keycaps have larger surface contact and larger finger touch area with ergonomic stepped design. Long-term use is not easy to fatigue, with better comfort.

✅【PBT Heat Sublimation Keycaps】Compared with ABS, PBT has better high hardness, wear resistance and high temperature resistance. The color has penetrated into the keycap, and the service life is up to ten years without fading.

✅【Unique Design】This set of keycaps has a unique autumn maple leaves design. Autumn gives people a comfortable and peaceful atmosphere, making you feel calm when using the keyboard, improving work efficiency and increasing the winning rate of games.

✅【What You Get】1x70 PBT Keycap set (key puller included), Keycaps only, The keyboard in the picture is only for the demo and is not for sale. If you have any questions about the size of the keycap of your keyboard, please feel free to confirm with us.

reviews.title: Nice
reviews.text: Overall these are really nice keycaps that show off the light underneath well. However a few keys did have some small blemishes on the top side. The blemishes are hidden between the keys and only exists on the upper solid color portion.

reviews.title: Terrible QC, all keycaps had rough bumps
reviews.text:
I just got my white set in the mail today and they would be good keycaps if there had been better quality control. It's like they took a bunch of flawed pieces and sold them to us as a set. Like another user said, I expected the shift, caps lock and tab keys to have the words spelled out and not just arrows.

There are rough bumps and weirdly broken pieces of plastic at the top of every keycap, as you can see on my picture. It is very hard to read the keys with backlight on since the font is so thin. The keyboard photographs well but it's very hard to see what key you are pressing. I feel deceived by the company and wish I had bought the HyperX ones instead, even though I didn't like the font.

reviews.title: doesnt work correctly for gmmk tkl
reviews.text: bought these to replace the stabilizers in my gmmk tk; board ... for some reason they just dont work.  they dont correctly click in and if you put a keycap on it and take the keycap off it pulls the whole thing with it. ended up just using the gold bars and white switch part with the other stuff from my gmmk.  its better than stock at least.

reviews.title: Everglides and they are nice
reviews.text: very nice Stabilizers,  just needsa touch of lube and they are good to go

reviews.title: Cure clicky stabs
reviews.text:
Since you always gotta replace PCB manufacturer stabs, you might as well buy these. Great quality. Highly recommend full Holee mod for the best feel. Always lube your stabs folks.
highly recommend.

reviews.title: Improvement on V1
reviews.text: I have used the first version of these and they work good, but have had issues with the stabilizer wires popping out when removing keycaps.  These updated V2 have extra hook to keep the wire in place and work nicely.  These are some of the best stabilizers you can get right now.

reviews.title: I should have gotten these a while ago...
reviews.text:
I have an NK87 and the stabilizer ticking was terrible. I did all the tricks for it, but it persisted from some keys. NK sells their stabilizers for about the same price as these, so I thought I would just have to live with it. Then I just decided to get these, and am so glad I did. Ticking is gone.

Key points
1. They look extremely similar to the NKs, but there is obviously some difference.
2. Straight and flat wires - no tuning needed
3. Manufactured right - i.e. no need to clip, stems are flat
4. Came with the soulmate tuning kit. I think this helped a lot too.

Highly recommended.

reviews.title: Best keycaps I have ever used.
reviews.text:
Bought these for my GMMK pro.

I have owned several mechanical keyboards, but never bought a high-end set of keycaps. Unlike every other set I've ever used, these just seem to ignore dirt and grease. I can stuff my face with a big Mac and go straight to typing, and the caps stay completely clean. I don't know how that works but I won't question it.

Other keyboards require weekly wipe downs, I have never had to do it with these caps.

They're also noticeably built well. These are going to outlast my fingers by a hundred years, likely.

reviews.title: Legend doesn’t glow like other pics
reviews.text: For my Tofu65, I do like these pudding key caps, but I’m not sure why the legends on my key caps aren’t transparent. Everyone who posted reviews with pics show their legends glowing, so I’m not sure why mine are different. My other issue is I got several duplicative keys, and I’m missing a few. For example, I only have one home key and about 4 page down keys. So that’s slightly annoying. I’ve seen some others complain about this similar thing as well. But the feel and look of these key caps are quality. Slightly disappointed.

reviews.title: Great caps work as advertised
reviews.text: Works great

reviews.title: for apex pro
reviews.text: bought these for my apex pro. they fit perfect and i definitely like them better than the caps that came with the keyboard

reviews.title: Great keys for $25
reviews.text: I really like these keys because it is very nicely built. The plastic is thick and there is some texture. The only problem with the texture part is that the A,W,S,D buttons are going to wear down and it will smooth out. I would appreciate if they can do something to make it more durable. I’d still recommend this for putty caps.

reviews.title: Great keycaps
reviews.text: i got these for my Apex Pro they look amazing on it about the only thing about them is more about the apex then the keycaps,the space bar only has one led on the keyboard in the center so does not light up the spacebar as much as the rest of the keys and is pretty noticeable...the kit came with everything needed to be used on several different keyboards that i have so i might get another set for my corsair boards next.

reviews.title: They look so good!
reviews.text: Made my Steelseries Apex 7 keyboard pop even more. They feel great with my Red Linear Switches. They are a little more expensive than some other brands, but I feel like it was better to go with the same brand since I have seen other people have issues with switching out the keycaps on Steelseries keyboards.

reviews.title: Love these!
reviews.text: Amazing PBT pudding keycaps, I absolutely love these! I chose these to match my SteelSeries keyboard and I'm very happy with them.

reviews.title: It looks so much cooler!
reviews.text: These are way cooler than the standard cords! Just look at them guys.

reviews.title: Beautiful cable, working perfectly
reviews.text: I've wanted a coiled cable for a while but they're all so expensive. Found this epomaker one and I've bought products from epomaker before and haven't had any issues, and since this cable was only $20 I figured I could risk it. I was even happier when I saw they had a lavender option, most places just have light pink but not lavender. This cable matches my keycaps perfectly. The coiled part lays flat right out of the box (I've seen a few from other companies where the coiled part kinda curls up, which I didnt want), the cable feels nice and sturdy and good quality, and the metal connector also feels nice and heavy. I also like that this is a woven cable and not just plastic/rubber wrapped. Had no issues with the keyboard working after plugging in the cable. Absolutely worth the $20, I might buy the light pink one to match my other keyboards.

reviews.title: Nice keys, font size a little horsey
reviews.text:
Note: I chose the Grey/Beige set. This set (YMDK 96 84 ANSI ISO Key) went on just fine, and are a great frugal find.

I bought these for an relatively inexpensive TKL mechanical wireless keyboard (RK87G) because the OEM keycaps were set in a truly fugly and illegible typeface.

These YMDKs feel good, and have a slightly deeper "thunk" when they bottom out. The two-character keys are typeset vertically, as Underwood and Remington intended. Although the font size on most of the keys is a big, they are fine and give the keyboard a bit of a homemade retro look.

NOTE Specific to the RK87G: This YMDK set does not have a ScrlLk key. The right Shift and right CTRL on this set are a little too small, so I just left the old ones on. I also left the OEM ScrlLk, Page Up, and arrow keys on because they have symbols for controlling the RGB LEDs. If you use the RKs function keys for app and control shortcuts, you may want to leave those on as well.

May 30, 2021
I've had these for a little over six months now. Still a great value set the money. I installed them on a keyboard I use for hours on end nearly every day. The printing on the beige keys hasn't faded, but I've noticed that the grey keys with white printing are beginning to fade. I'm not taking any stars off because before I purchased these I had already read other reviewers who had the same experience.

reviews.title: Good for the money
reviews.text: It didn’t give me the thoc sound that I wanted but given the price it is good enough

reviews.title: Rough surface, washed out colors
reviews.text:
These caps seems well made enough, with a rough texture that provides ample grip without having a sandpaper effect on your fingers. The colors are a fair bit less saturated than the images make them look. Slightly annoying but they're still quite nice.

Do note that this set does not come with a long shift or scroll lock cap. This isn't an issue as they'll be going on a 65% for me but if you're looking to put them on a full size board you may be disappointed.

reviews.title: good value
reviews.text: satisfied with this purchase although the caps do feel a tad bit rough

title: ASCENY Coiled & Double-Sleeved Mechanical Keyboard Cable, for Type-C Mechanical Keyboards (Purple)
description: As MK enthusiasts who are obsessed with building keyboards, we understood the needs of the mechanical keyboards community. This cable is our latest take on designed mechanical keyboard cables. Our goal was to create a mass production product that will allow more people to get into the hobby without having to spend a huge amount of money. After many failed attempts to make a great coil in mass production, we finally believe that we have created a great product that stands in one with the standards of mechanical keyboard enthusiasts.
features: Stiff plastic injected Asceny connectors. No more amateurish heatshrinks.
PET double sleeve that gives a beautiful texture, and extra durability.
4.9ft long (excluding the coil length) & Super flexible 6" tight coil that lasts.
Comes in a beautiful package that holds the coil tight. Can be saved and re-used whenever you want to store your cable.

reviews.title: Great cable for a small investment.
reviews.text:
Being new to to the Mechanical Keyboard market, I recently purchased a prebuilt "budget" board that came with a long plain usb-c cable, which would be fine but I wanted a cleaner look for my desk. Aviator cables are great but I did not want to invest $50+ for a single use cable. In comes Asceny with a cable that costs half as much and looks just as good in my opinion. The packaging was nicely made, and does not feel cheap in any way, this company really put a lot of love into the product.

My only complaint is that with the direction of the coil, I can not have the Asceny logo facing up with a left side charge port on my keyboard, otherwise the cable does not sit correctly on the desk. This would not dissuade me from recommending it though, and may even encourage some buyers.

reviews.title: Keychron K4 Compatible
reviews.text:
Read reviews about the plug not fitting on some Keychrons but was prepared to modify. Turns out, it fits just fine, and I haven’t had any issues so far.
Cable itself feels solid and durable, a bit stiff, but maneuverable.
Overall, for the price, I’d say go with it if you are debating.

reviews.title: Nice
reviews.text: Good value, sturdy, nice style

reviews.title: Good starter coil cable for keyboard build
reviews.text: This is my first coil keyboard cable for my first mechanical keyboard build. I can’t compare it to one of the more popular brands like Glorious for example, but for what it is, it’s a nice, sturdy, well made cable. It looks good and is a nice accessory for my keyboard. The coils are actually smaller than I thought they would be, but still nice. I wish the cable was longer though, that’s my only complaint.

reviews.title: good wire to save space and for the *aesthetic*
reviews.text: i love how the coiled-wire pairs with the mechanical keyboard. it makes my desk look less messy and looks fab LOL

reviews.title: Cool
reviews.text: Cool

title: Akko Keycaps Set, Black&Bronze Theme 158-Key ASA Profile PBT Double-Shot Keycap Set with Black Keycaps for Mechanical Keyboards with Collection Box
features: BLACK&BRONZE THEME – The combination with classic black, gray and dark bronze,　the Black&Bronze keycaps brings heavy sense of history. The bronze charactors shine on the dark keycaps let us fantasy the bronze gleams in the dark night through thick fog.

ULTIMATE CUSTOMIZATION FLEXIBILITY – Comes with Akko ASA profile, the 158-key keycap set is suitable for a variety of layouts including but not limited to 61-key(60%), 68-key (65%), 84-key (75%), 87-key (TKL), 98-Key (Classic 1800), 100-Key (96%) and full 108-key size, and thus offers strong flexibility for keyboard personalization.
PBT DOUBLE-SHOT – Double-shot keycaps are extremely durable and resistant to wear even for decades, and the legends are particularly vibrant, sharp with high contrast.

ASA PROFILE – Akko’s proprietary ASA profile is tailored to offer a more familiar and comfortable typing experience by matching the height for OEM profile while maintaining the aesthetics and spheric top of the classic SA profile.

FOR MX STRUCTURE SWITCHES – Compatible with MX structure switches including but not limited to Cherry, Gateron, Akko and Kailh etc. Large keys of non-standard layout & stabilizers may not be compatible.

reviews.title: Some of the best keycaps under $100
reviews.text:
These keycaps are fantastic. The quality of material is noticeable right when you open the package, and the layout compatibility is uncomparable. For about $60 you get keycaps that work with the following layouts:

Full Size
1800
96%
75%
65%
60%
40% (non ortholinear)

The only layouts that aren't supported are ortholinear (though you might be able to make something work) and split (no split spacebar here). 60% with arrows (the 64-key) also is not supported as there is no 2u shift.

The colors are fantastic, the material feels very nice, and the cap profile is just incredibly comfortable to type on. In fact, it's so comfortable the only caps that I think are nicer are Drop's MT3 which cost double and don't come with as much layout variation without buying extra kits.

This specific set, Dracula's Castle, also comes with custom novelites and two different colors worth of compatibility (crimson and black) for a lot of the keys to make your board look exactly the way you want.

I'd highly recommend anything in ASA profile. The Cherry profile is good too, but not s comfortable for me to type on personally.

reviews.title: So amazing for the price
reviews.text: They looks so good for the price, Akko does it again!

reviews.title: Great quality double shot pbt keycaps.
Pretty Great Set with an Interesting Profile
reviews.text: This set is pretty nice, unlike most of Akko's keycap sets this set doesn't directly rip off color ways from existing GMK or EBPT sets, it comes close to something like SA Bliss but it adds black mods so it's not exactly the same.  The keycaps themselves are fairly nice and the font of the legends are a lot more consistent than their cherry profile sets. The packing is nice albeit a little frustrating as you pretty much need to use a keycap puller to remove the keycaps. The ASA profile is what I found the most interesting, if I had to compare it to anything I would actually compare it to OEM profile but slightly taller with rounded edges than something SA profile, The sculpting is a lot more derivative of OEM than SA in my opinion. The typing feel is pretty great it is very reminiscent of OEM profile so if you are used to OEM profile you will be right at home. The sound is more clacky than thocky at least in my NK65 with Gateron Cap Gold Switches. Overall the keycaps are nice and the ASA profile is a pleasant surprise coming from someone that uses both SA and OEM profile fairly regularly.

reviews.title: Good quality, Vivid colors, Nice sounding, Great value
reviews.text:
Akko Los Angeles 158-Key ASA Profile PBT Double-Shot Full Keycap Set for Mechanical Keyboards with Collection Box

I really love the profile of these keycaps. I have a hard time with whether to call this company a knock off company or not. They do have their own color combos and make combos that other companies do for a lot cheaper. Are color combos and fonts really proprietary? I'm not sure and eventually there will be no more combination of colors that one company will be able to claim as only their colors. I do think that Akko make really good keycaps and you get a lot for your money. So that's my two cents.

title: DROP + Matt3o MT3 Susuwatari Custom Keycap Set, ABS Hi-Profile Keycaps, Doubleshot Legends, Cherry MX Compatible Set for Ortholinear Mechanical Keyboards (Ortholinear)
features: A GROUNDBREAKING PROFILE, BACK FOR MORE: Sculpted in Hi-Profile, Matt3o’s groundbreaking MT3 /dev/tty custom keycap set changed the way we type.

FROM THE IBM TERMINAL TO THE DESKTOP: Based on the profile form the iconic IBM beamspring terminals of the 1970s, this custom set is fully sculpted in Hi-Profile. From its heavily slanted design to the scooped, contoured tops of each cap, the set recreates the feel of vintage keyboards.

DOUBLESHOT LEGENDS: As opposed to a dye-sublimiation process, these doubleshot legends are permanently molded inside the keycap, thus becoming an integral part of it. This ensures reliable legends and continuity throughout the set.
ORTHOLINEAR: For ortho keyboards like the Drop Planck, Preonic, and other boards with similar layouts.
SPECS: Produced by Drop and designed by Matt3o, ABS Hi-Profile MT3 keycaps, Doubleshot legends, MX compatible, and Fully sculpted based on IBM beamspring keycaps.

reviews.title: Absolute necessity on ortholinear boards
reviews.text:
These keycaps are thick, double shot so they will never fade, and sculpted. With ortholinear keyboards, these keycaps really help adjusting to smaller modifer key size and different key layout.

Pros:
1. Thick: This helps lower the pitch of the sound, which makes it sound quieter.
2. Doubleshot ABS: No wearing out the legends. The font is also fairly thick, making it much more legible than some other keycaps.
3. MT3 profile: The sculpted profile really makes it very comfortable to use and helps reduce hitting the wrong keys. No sharp edges due to the spherical shape. Very enjoyable for typing. The profile is much higher though so that could be an issue for some people.
4. Multiple profile options for certain keys: For example, the Ctrl and Esc keycs come in profiles that would allow it to be the top row or 3rd row. Space bar comes as two 1u, two 1.5u, and one 2u.
5. Storage tray and box: This seems silly to put as a pro but the tray and box it comes with make it easy to store unused or keycaps it replaced. Other Keycaps come in a zip lock bag that will just be a jumbled mess.

Cons:
1. Price. This is really expensive for a set of keycaps but this is the normal price for any specialty keycaps. It is what it is.
2. Limitation of scultped profile: Putting key profiles meant for row 1 in row 3 feels awkward so you might end you designing your keymap partially based on keycap profiles. Also might end up rotating some keycaps like row 5 Raise or Lower layer keycaps to make it more comfortable.
3. ABS option only. The PBT version is in white only. So if you prefer the color with the material that it isn't made it, then you're out of luck.
4. Limited to buying sets if you need extra keys: If you need more keys, you have to order a set. I would like to have more 1.5u blank keycaps but don't want to buy multiple sets just for the few pieces I want.

reviews.title: I like them
reviews.text:
These are my daily drivers, I use them on a Drop CTRL with Glorious Panda switches. Initially I thought the MT3 profile was disorienting, but after using them for a while, I am quite fond of it. I acknowledge that not everyone will like this profile though, and that should be considered if you are buying this product (or any like it). If you can find an MT3 set to try first before dumping about two hundred dollars on keycaps, it would be a **very good** idea. Other things go without saying, the thickness is very good the weight on my switches feels good and not like its making keys too easy to press or anything. I really like the theme, but all of that considered, I bought this specifically for the VI keys (hjkl with arrows) because I thought they looked super cool.

The price for these keycaps is a good deterrent for most people, for good reason. However this high of a price is pretty standard for high quality keycaps, as well as the Drop.com-exclusive MT3 profile.

This leads me to the value for money, its expensive and definitely should only be bought if you have a hankering to spend money on some expensive keycaps or you really really like having a specific row of keycaps.
Brightness is a 1 star because they're not shine-through keycaps, as they should be.
Gaming is a 4 star because, like, Sure? nothing about these keycaps keep you from gaming.

reviews.title: Best 3rd party caps I've ever owned.
reviews.text:
I really liked the esthetics of these key caps because I was looking for something which resembled "Old Nasa" feel, and these were the best I could find. The ergonomics were also great and everything has a very nice and snug fit, unlike the original keycaps that came with my CTRL keyboard.

I only wish that the "additional" buttons came with the normal key caps for the money I spent on the original "package". $30 for about 10 extra keys I can use on my ten keyless is VERY steep.

reviews.title: Best keycap set
reviews.text: I love this set. Worth every penny.

reviews.title: Absolutely gorgeous
reviews.text: Wonderful keycaps , goes great on my Nyquist Keyboard, looks professional yet custom, I highly recommend.

reviews.title: AMAZING!
reviews.text: i've recently bought a bunch of resin keycap's & by far this one key from this seller is hands down NO CORNER'S CUT on making these KEYCAPS because out of about $1k spent on custom keycap's this one hands down is still the best one hands down all the way to the clearness of the key, how much of the RGB of the key show's threw, even the blue & pink look's amazingly vibrant key is super smooth just nothing i'd do different besides maybe send a better box 2 be able to store these caps in because its almost like a diamond ring or set of nice ear ring's u want to be able to have its own box an be able 2 display it weather its on ur keyboard or shelve/display case.

reviews.title: Really looks nice!
reviews.text: Looks really nice, not really that great at allowing RGB to shine through but overall I think it's a nice, beautiful addition to any keyboard.  Mine fit perfectly and adds a certain nice uniqueness to a keyboard that I, as an artist, really enjoy.  I'd definitely recommend, this is really nice looking.

reviews.title: Really Nice Artisan, But May Fit Tightly
reviews.text: The quality of this artisan is really good for the price, and it looks quite nice when illuminated by backlighting as well. The keycap does fit quite tightly but I did manage to get it to fit though some may have issues with fitment. Overall it's a nice artisan but may fit tightly so be aware of that when ordering.

Designed & Produced by FINEDAY, Every details are designed by FINEDAY

reviews.title: Great for the look, 10 key A plus, Functional enough for device swap
reviews.text:
I really wanted this keyboard for the typewriter look, device swap, and 10-key.

The only setback is that even when plugged in via USB to the main device when waking up there is a few seconds of buffering time before being able to type at times. At first, it drove me crazy but now I notice that the green light signals when this is happening and goes solid when ready which alleviates the "let's go" anxiety because I know what's happening. I love that it pairs to 3 devices. 2 via Bluetooth and one via usb.

Another weird thing is maybe at least 1 time a week I have to unplug and plug the keyboard back in when I cannot type on my device and I know that the correct device is selected and the solid green light is on. If there were a better typewriter-style keyboard in all black and chrome with 10-key and pairs to at least 3 devices that don't have the buffering when swapping device or intermittent typing issue, I would have picked it but it doesn't exist.

Overall 4 stars.

Update:

Key caps are already falling apart after 3 months use! My heart is so heavy it’s so noticeable and I cannot find a replacement key hundreds of dollars down the drain.

reviews.title: Keys are dim
reviews.text: I sent mine back because I couldnt see the keys when I was 2 feet away. The backlight shines more between the keys than through them. It was ok in a very dark room but a dim indoor setting they were impossible to see unless you were right over the keyboard, even with the light turned all the way up. They were also kind of wobbly, cant see anyone typing fast on this. I bought a querty writer and the quality difference is huge.

reviews.title: Love the retro feel and "noise"
reviews.text: Love the retro feel and clacking noise.  Typing accuracy has improved!  The ability to switch between 3 devices is extremely convenient!

reviews.title: Spacebars do not fit well
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:14b0c07be1bf5b90e20f388d925ce8c8]] The keycap set is great in terms of design, modifier key size options, and key legend for quality for price. Fitting for all keys is great, excepts for the large spacebars.
The space bars (both 6.25u and 7u) in my set don’t work smoothly and somehow get the stabs stuck (two different kinds of stabs as well). I tried them on two keyboards (one with 7u and other with 6.25u) and only these spacebars showed problems and the other spacebars I have do not show this problem. I am not sure really whats causing the space bar issue, maybe those pbt space keycaps are slightly bent (as I have read about) but visually they look okay.

reviews.title: Good Quality for the $ - Fits Cherry MX Switches
reviews.text:
This fit my Ducky mechanical keyboard without issue. They seem to be well made and fit tightly in my cherry MX switches. A couple observations, not sure it’s a positive or a negative but a point of observation:

1) They do not come with media key replacements. The blue ones you see in my image are from my original key caps

2) The height of the keys are slightly shorter than my Ducky OEMs. While initially this was a possible concern for me, having typed with the new keys for a couple weeks, seems to not impact typing at all.

3) The blue caps that came with the replacements are not the ones you see in my photo (Enter key). They have a slightly yellow / blue hue to them vs. the Ducky’s I’m using. Wanted my enter key to match the rest of the blue media keys so I mixed and matched. Some may like the blue that comes with the set, I just preferred my original blue keys color.

For the price, they fit the bill, can’t complain. We’ll see long term how the printing of the keys holds up / how quickly they wear down.

Wish list:

- Caps lock key had the word ‘Caps’ on it
- Windows button keys had something besides “Super”.

Overall good purchase.

title: DROP + Invyr Holy Panda Mechanical Keyboard Switches - Plate Mounted Tactile Switches, Cherry MX Style, 67g Spring, by Kailh (70 PCS)
features: THE MOST TACTILE SWITCHES IN THE WORLD: With a name like Holy Panda, these switches had to be epic. Made with stems from Halo switches, housing found on Invyr Panda switches, Holy Panda switches combine the best of both worlds.
THE BEST OF BOTH WORLDS: The result is a snappy, tactile switch that is satisfying to type on.

COMPATIBILITY: Holy Panda switches are compatible with plate-mounted Cherry-style PCB's and are perfect for hot-swap compatible barebones kits such as the Drop ALT, CTRL, SHIFT, ENTR, Carina, Planck, and Preonic.
SPECS: Halo Switch Stems, Polycarbonate top housing, Nylon bottom housing, 67g spring, Plate Mount, and MX-compatible.

reviews.title: Excellent tactile
reviews.text:
I have purchased these, lubed Glorious Panda, and Tecsee Purple panda.

This is the best with the other 2 being very close to as good as this. The tactile-ness is a little better than the other both. With the other 2, the tactile is right at the beginning, while this one starts at the beginning and seems to feel the same until bottoming out. All have the nice bump back.

I didn't lube these and they sound and feel great. I think this is my favorite tactile now. But if you're on budget, I would recommend one of the others.

reviews.title: Not a bad one in the bunch
reviews.text: Got the 90 pack and not a broken or bent pin in the entire bunch. Professionally packed and working 100 percent. All switches tested and working fine. A little expensive but worth it for the fast Amazon delivery.

title: YUNZII AKKO CS Switch, 3 Pins for DIY MX Mechanical Keyboard, 45 pcs (Ocean Blue Switch)
features: 【About AKKO CS Switches】 AKKO Custom Series (CS) switches are the newly designed 3-pin switches for customers who have specific typing and gaming needs. Each package has 45 switches. Six types of switches are available and no matter which kind of typing feeling you want, we cover it all.

【Compatibility】AKKO CS switches are designed with a general MX structure so that they can fit most keycaps sets. The switch has 3 pins but also fit in all 3 pins and 5 pins keyboards. Based on the strong compatibility, you can easily change the switch and customize your mechanical keyboard.
【Built-in LED Slot 】The AKKO CS switches are equipped with a north-facing LED slot for LED modification. It is also compatible with SMD LED, provides more fun and options for keyboard enthusiasts.

【Durable Lifespan & Improved Performance】With a lifespan of approximately 50 million times of keystrokes, you are assured of high quality and a long lifespan. The CS switches feature a spiral spring design that can make the typing more comfortable.

【Ocean Blue Tactile Switch】The tactile bump of the Ocean Blue switch is at 0.55mm and it ends at 1.88mm, which gives long-continued feedback. Different from the blue and brown switch, the Ocean blue switch has a medium tactile position that can give you an excellent experience in typing and gaming.

reviews.title: Very good for the price
reviews.text: I'm not a mechanical keyboard connoisseur, but needed to replace the switches on my cheap Redragon keyboard to get rid of the loud clicking noise. The original switches were blue switch clones and the clicks will wake up the neighbors. These are cheap enough that I decided to give them a try. They are pretty good in terms of touch and sound, from my perspective and my use. Will definitely resort to these again in the future if I need to replace keyboard switches, especially for budget keyboards.

description:
Note:
It is shiny but Not fingerprint proof. Please do not use any sharp things to rub it, or the golden color may fade.  Keycaps only, NO Keyboard included.

reviews.title: Not doublshot
reviews.text:
These two keys: < and > have already worn through. These are not double shot keycaps. They are a semi-translucent keycap with a finish over it that wears through. Doubleshot has an injection molded insert inside the keycap with the translucent area cut out of the keycap.
Should you not care about that, here is some more info. They look okay in the light, but in the dark there is a strong screen glare off of them that can be distracting.

I rate one star for lying about the nature of the product.

reviews.title: eh, cheap.  removed them almost instantly.
reviews.text: slippery, their feel is very cheap and i ended up removing them and regretting my buy.  i have bought other replacment keys before.

reviews.title: I didn't try them...
reviews.text: The color wasn't what I expected so I didn't open the very nice plastic packaging before returning.

reviews.title: DOES NOT FIT CORSAIR STRAFE RGB.
reviews.text: Overall, it's pretty cool. I like it a lot. It is very smudge, and the foil is cheap so it wont last all that long.

reviews.title: Arrived missing key caps but overall pretty good if you have the right keyboard
reviews.text:
Not a keyboard, just key caps to "bling out" your existing keyboard.

I purchased the following keyboard and these key caps fit perfectly. The keyboard (not this item, which is only key caps) comes with a red plastic tool that you can use to pop out the key caps:

Redragon K551 Mechanical Gaming Keyboard RGB LED Rainbow Backlit Wired Keyboard with Red Switches for Windows Gaming PC (104 Keys, Black)

Even the font on the key caps matches the keyboard I bought separately, and the backlighting shines through the font.

The only down side: the set of key caps is missing about 1/4 of the keys (see attached picture).

I would give it all five stars if it had arrived containing all the key caps advertised in the product listing. It was a surprise prank gift for a coworker, which is why I made do with what we got.

title: KINESIS USB-A Freestyle Pro Quiet Ergonomic Split Mechanical Keyboard (Cherry MX Silent Red Switches)
features: AWARD-WINNING SPLIT DESIGN: Advanced ergonomics for all body types and preferences via adjustable split, splay, and tent (tenting accessories sold separately). New cable storage compartment supports up to 20" of separation when you need it.

GENUINE CHERRY MECHANICAL SWITCHES FOR PREMIUM TYPING FEEL AND PERFORMANCE WITH LESS NOISE: Cherry MX "Silent" red mechanical switches feature a low activation force, linear feel and are rated for 50M key presses for unmatched durability.

FULL ONBOARD PROGRAMMABILITY WITH THE DRIVERLESS SMARTSET ENGINE: 9 custom layouts, 8 office hotkeys, on-the-fly macro recording, one-touch key remapping, pre-configured Mac and Dvorak layouts, settings saved to keyboards 4MB flash memory, and the SmartSet App GUI for Windows & Mac.
PLUG-AND-PLAY WITH ALL MAJOR OPERATING SYSTEMS: Windows 7-10, Mac OS X, Linux, Chrome.
2 YEAR MANUFACTURER'S WARRANTY: Buy with confidence knowing that Kinesis engineers all our products in the USA to the highest standards and stands behind them 100 percent since 1992.

reviews.title: Good, but could be better
reviews.text:
I use this with my MacBook Pro. I have brutal pain in my hands, wrists and forearms from osteoarthritis, tendon-patchy and tendonitis; caused from 19 years of being at my computer and holding my phone up for blogging.

This keyboard has made things so much better, however... I needed the keyboard to have the lift without the wrist pads which are agony for my hands. Only, the tent system is made to include all pieces; the lift and the wrist pads. So, I have to use the lift pieces without the wrist pads which leaves a chunk of awkward plastic sticking straight out from the keyboard; something I am constantly catching. It's frustrating to no end.

I am now looking for something I can simply glue to the inner, underside of the keyboard that will add the lift but leave the front open.

I'm also disappointed with the price. Come on now. It's sooooo overpriced. It's only a matter of time before another brand comes out with a more reasonable one- but in the meantime, I feel like they are taking advantage.

In the end, this is far better than nothing. It definitely places your hands in a more ergonomic position, and is better than a flat keyboard or laptop touchpad setup.

reviews.title: Great keyboard for ergonomics
reviews.text: The split keyboard layout has been great for my shoulders and arms.  It took a couple of weeks for me to get used to the layout but the extra functions offered on the edges of the keyboard are very helpful.  Being able to replace the key caps is huge.  I really love this keyboard.

reviews.title: Quality material, but as wide as TKL keyboards
reviews.text:
I wish the length of the cable was longer, and that a longer cable can be purchased separately.
For the price, the tenting should be standard, but it is a separate purchase. And, without them, this keyboard isn't as great.

I purchased the tenting separately, and it really helps with ergonomics when typing. But, when gaming it tends to push my hands side and down, so it's better to use a separate palm support.

I was surprised that when the keyboard is laid out, it is as wide as a ten keyless keyboard. I was hoping that it would be shorter, so that the mouse has more room. It's fine for work or just typing. For gaming, I'll put the right section farther up. Or, I'll put it above the keyboard tray, so I still have access to the keys when needed. That frees up a lot of room for the mouse.

The quality of the keyboard is great. Everything feels solid. The keys feel wonderful. And, the ergonomics is great for typing.
The tech support was friendly and helpful. I had a bit of a problem early on with customizing the keys, and their tech support promptly helped me.

reviews.title: Great keyboard
reviews.text: Nice quiet keyboard with a great feel.  I got the tenting and wrist rest kit.  It took a little bit to get used to the angle but I'm really enjoying it and have already programmed some of the keys to do routine things for me.

reviews.title: Perfect balance of ergonomics and key comfort.
reviews.text:
I have tried all the Kinesis keyboards with the exception of the 400$ console setup. I have chosen this one as the best overall experience, specifically paired with the VIP3Pro tent and wrist accessory. The keys on this keyboard have a much more consistent and pleasant feeling as compared to their membrane offerings as well as their silent switch option. Those keyboards are fantastic if you are not already addicted to the feeling of mechanical keys. However if you are searching for the ultimate in keyboard experiences for daily use, I heavily and without reservation recommend this keyboard set. The keyboard may initially seem expensive when compared to other ergonomic keyboards that do not split or offer the tenting/wrist rests, however having tried many different curved and non split ergonomic options from Microsoft, Logitech, and many others, I can say without a doubt that the optimal configuration requires the keyboard to be split. If you need to have a number pad like I do, I suggest any simple number key option, they'll fit nicely between the two halves and do not interfere at all. I plan to purchase a second keyboard for my WFH setup to compliment my work setup. I don't want to go back to a standard keyboard and you won't either after using it.

This review was not endorsed, sponsored, or influenced by any 3rd party, I am simply that overjoyed with the product.

title: Akko Keycaps Top Printed Shine-Through with ASA Profile, White Keycaps PBT Double-Shot Keycaps for Mechanical Keyboards
features: SHINE-THROUGH KEYCAPS – Deliver premium and shining RGB experiences with Akko ASA Shine-through keycap sets! Come with extremely durable and resistant double-shot Shine-through keycaps, the legend shine through keycaps brings unique and great RGB effects.

ULTIMATE CUSTOMIZATION FLEXIBILITY – Comes with Akko ASA profile, the keycap set is suitable for a variety of layouts including 61-key(60%), 64-key, 68-key (65%), 84-key (75%), 87-key (TKL), 98-Key (Classic 1800), 100-Key (96%), Alice and full 108-key size, and thus offers strong flexibility for keyboard personalization.

ASA PROFILE – Akko’s proprietary ASA profile is tailored to offer a more familiar and comfortable typing experience by matching the height for OEM profile while maintaining the aesthetics and spheric top of the classic SA profile.
PBT DOUBLE-SHOT – Double-shot keycaps are extremely durable and resistant to wear even for decades, and the legends are particularly vibrant, sharp with high contrast.

FOR MX STRUCTURE SWITCHES – Compatible with MX structure mechanical switches and mainstream keyboards including but not limited to Cherry, Gateron, Akko and Kailh. Large keys of non-standard layout & stabilizers may not be compatible.

reviews.title: Not the brightest shine-through, but for the material and price it's worth it.
reviews.text:
Akko's ASA profile is a taller profile kind of like SA. Feels great to type on and is fine for gaming if you don't mind the extra travel time on each keystroke. The finish on these has a great texture and it's PBT so it shouldn't wear out quickly. "Shouldn't" being relative since I tend to shred keycaps as though my fingers were first dipped in acid before typing.

30.00 for keycaps of this quality and for the number of keys is great especially in a hobby where everything is over priced, over hyped, and overly bogged down in group buys and pre-orders that take literal years to fulfill.

reviews.title: Shine-Through keycaps, look amazing
reviews.text: These shine-through keycaps look awesome.  They are super easy to install, like most keycaps.  It is just a bit time consuming.  I love being able to change out keys and switches and seem to collect keycaps.  These are great and sound and feel amazing.

title: 68 Keycap PBT OEM Height Five-Sided Sublimation Compatible with GH60 / GK64 / GK61 / 68/87/104/108 Mechanical Keyboard with Cherry MX Switch Purple Tang Dynasty 64 Keys
features: 【Widely compatible】And this keycap set can be used with most60/ 61/64/64 keyboard designs that support left shift, Cherry MX switch mechanical gaming keyboard and Outemu, Kailh and Gateron switches, and fit most standard ANSI keyboards.

【PBT dye sublimation process】These 64-key keycaps are made of high-quality PBT material, with a matte texture and anti-grease coating, which can provide first-class durability and typing feel.Advanced five-sided sublimation technology makes the pattern not fade.
【Diversification Keycap Selection】Consists of 64 keycaps and DIY keycaps, suitable for all mechanical keyboards，as well as any key switch with a cross-shaped axis，Ideal for DIY mechanical keyboard.
【Specification】PBT Keycaps designed with 6.25U space bar, the thickness is around 1.5mm,The exquisite pattern is perfectly combined with the PBT keycap.
【Commodity keycap】The product is a keycap set. The keyboard in the picture is only for displaying keycaps, not for sale.

reviews.title: Clean key caps
reviews.text: Using these key caps on a Corsair K70 pro mini wireless and they look great. They feel good and match my purple and white theme
